title: narcissus bulbocodium subsp. hedraeanthus
intro: Narcissus bulbocodium subsp. hedraeanthus, also known as the small-flowered winter aconite, is a plant species that belongs to the Amaryllidaceae family, characterized by its small white or pale yellow flowers and narrow, sword-shaped leaves. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including its potential antiviral and antibacterial effects. The small-flowered winter aconite is believed to have health benefits such as reducing inflammation and fighting off infections, as well as being used as an expectorant to relieve respiratory issues. The plant is often prepared as a tincture, tea, or infusion for its medicinal uses, although its preparation and consumption should be done under the guidance of a healthcare professional.
